reject_drivers: Phase-2 FAIL VC-18 (#275 anchor pin loop does not cover references/*.md)

fix_decisions.note: guard divergence closed by the same single-sourcing
one-shot-per-key documented + test added (documentation was the reviewer's own stated alternative)
cmd_status defensive floor documented as unreachable

fix_decisions.claim_text: Critical: the running state has no lease/expiry, so a crashed owner wedges the flight permanently
fix_decisions.evidence: Observable fail-direction is fail-CLOSED: a wedged running handle makes every attacher take wait_expired and launch directly, so the cost is duplicate work, never a false pass. More decisively, the prescribed fix contradicts the issue's own AC: 'the ledger never infers that an unobserved process ended' - auto-expiring a running handle IS that inference. Recorded evaluation, not an auto-demotion; the deliberate design is now documented in-source at the attach seam.

fix_decisions.claim_text: cmd_claim's winning-owner write should use the temp-file + os.replace pattern like every other mutation, to avoid a torn read by a concurrent attacher
fix_decisions.evidence: The prescribed fix would break the single-owner guarantee it sits next to. O_CREAT|O_EXCL on the FINAL path IS the mutual-exclusion primitive that grants exactly one owner; os.replace overwrites unconditionally, so a temp+replace claim would let two concurrent callers both believe they own the flight. The torn-read window is real but already fail-closed: _read_flight classifies a partial read as ReadError('empty'/'malformed_json'), which is a non-pass and routes the attacher to a direct launch. Trading a bounded, safe, low-probability duplicate launch for a possible double-owner is the wrong direction.

parking_evidence.basis: the prescription contradicts the single-owner invariant the same function implements
parking_evidence.failing_input: two concurrent claim callers on one flight key
parking_evidence.source: scripts/verification-flight.py cmd_claim O_CREAT|O_EXCL branch and _read_flight's empty/malformed_json arms
